plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-315 RP09.P07.M04.S17-RP09.P07.M06.S14 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-315 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP09.P07.M04 secondary workflow failure tail (failure unit test, integration smoke, audit failure hint, Hermes failure evidence), the full M05 permission/audit binding failure cycle (22 rows including Claude edge-case prompt and human escalation note), and the M06 synthetic fixture head (14 rows) across three sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H09), Claude review packet (C09), and CP00-316 handoff, without opening rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, test runtime paths, integration smoke runtime, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-315 is a descriptor-only RP09 CRM failure tail-slice pack (40 units, RP09.P07.M04.S17-RP09.P07.M06.S14) and its claims are well supported by independent read-only inspection. Coverage and the deliverable/micro-title distributions reconcile exactly to 40 across the review baseline, the contract failure_tail_slice_case_set, the CRM_CORE_CP315_REQUIREMENTS constants in registry.js, and the validator. Every case-set row is descriptor-only with runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, and raw_payload_included:false; freezeCp315Result plus the contract/manifest no_write_attestation assert the full no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data surface (writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true, no DB/object-storage/audit/Hermes-runtime writes, deny_over_allow, no cross-tenant access, no leak of permission/audit/blocked-claim/lock/fixture/hermes detail). Three dedicated CP315 tests and validate-rp09-crm-core-contract.mjs (which deep-equals the contract failure_tail_slice_descriptor, cp315_requirements, and cp315_no_write_attestation against the registry constants) pass, and command-evidence records every expected validation command at exit 0 with git diff --check clean. The pack does NOT over-claim production readiness: manifest and command-evidence hold production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review, with production_ready_candidate:true scoped strictly to local validation and explicitly not an enterprise-trust claim. Authority boundaries are intact: Claude is read-only (tools Read,Grep,Glob), claude_is_final_approval:false, human final approval required before runtime opening (CP00-316), and CP178+ hardening lists all invalid-evidence blockers (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing refs/inspection-basis, final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims); readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). One cosmetic metadata inconsistency was noted in the review scaffolding (not the pack deliverables); it does not affect implementation, tests, boundaries, or the closeout decision. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Final enterprise-trust sign-off remains with the human approver; this review is advisory and not final approval.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own cp00-315 evidence dir and the session handoff doc
findings.details: The generated scope metadata lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-315/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). docs/closeout-packs/cp00-315/ is unambiguously this pack's own evidence directory and is in-scope, so its presence in the unrelated_dirty_files_preserved array is a cosmetic generator artifact rather than a true conflict. This is metadata in the review scaffolding only; the in-scope tracked product files are correctly and unambiguously enumerated in active_pack_implementation_refs, and no contract, source, test, attestation, or production_ready claim is affected. The instruction to not infer unrelated dirty files into the pack remains satisfied because the real unrelated files (the v2.0 .docx and the ldip-integration c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt JSON) are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the changed-file-scope generator to exclude the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack_id>/ directory (and pack-authored handoff docs) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so scope attribution is unambiguous. No change to pack deliverables is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not block pack or goal closeout. Human final approver may disregard. Tracked as a non-blocking tooling refinement.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-315 crm core failure tail slice descriptor pack
